The question is asking to identify the smallest margin by which the team won a match during the season. To find this, we can sort the "result" column, which contains numerical strings representing the match outcomes (e.g., "1-0", "2-1", "3-0"), in the order of "small to large". This will allow us to see the smallest winning margin (i.e., the closest win) first.

The question is asking to determine the smallest margin of victory (shortest number of points) the team had during the season. Since each row represents a game result, we need to analyze the "result" column to find the smallest difference between the team's points and the opponent's points. We can use the f_group_column(result) operation to group the results and then calculate the point differences to find the smallest margin.

The shortest number of points this team won by during this season is represented by the smallest margin of victory in the "result" column. The results with "1–0" indicate a one-point victory. This occurred 3 times during the season. Therefore, the shortest number of points won by is 1.
